reportText: Behavioural Patterns Apr 07-Jun 30, 2026 · 39 unique reports PEAK ENDURANCEPEAK SPEEDCORE PATTERN 60.166.1Agility held best Puzzles SolvingPolgar StudiesFocus used 87.0% of tracked time June 12 · 09:13 AMMay 25 · 07:02 PMWellness used 13.0%, other 0.0% No true content duplicates were found, but only 2 filename stems were reused across all 39 files. KEY INSIGHTS IN K ALL K All covers 39 unique sessions from April 07, 2026 through June 30, 2026, with no true duplicate content. The main data-quality problem is filename reuse: only 2 normalized filenames were used to store all 39 different reports. Polgar Studies was the fastest session at 66.1 speed, while Puzzles Solving had the best endurance at 60.1. Polgar Studies carried the highest agility at 61.4, reinforcing that agility often stayed stronger than raw speed. The slowest ramp-up appeared in Lecture at 368s, matching the broader pattern of startup friction across the folder. REPRESENTATIVE SESSIONS A high-signal cross-section of K All rather than the full 39-row ledger. SessionTimeDurationSpee d AgilityEnduran ce Reading Polgar StudiesMay 25 · 07:02 PM 30m 23s66.143.636.5Fastest session Puzzles SolvingJun 12 · 09:13 AM 54m 46s55.443.560.1Strongest stamina Polgar StudiesMay 21 · 06:53 PM 30m 20s54.361.448.1Best agility LectureApr 13 · 11:15 AM 1h 0m 20s21.849.657.8Largest ramp-up delay Classical Game Against Sanjay Jun 13 · 02:37 PM 4m 19s58.629.730.7Shortest sample Game Against RaghavJun 04 · 02:05 PM 1h 45m 18s 34.659.558.2Longest session PATTERN READ Directional findings across the full K All set. FILES The folder is unique by content but messy by filename All 39 files resolved to different session records. Only 2 normalized filename stems were reused across them. TIME MIX Focus work dominated tracked time Focus sessions covered 20h 26m 12s of tracked time. Wellness sessions covered 3h 3m 40s and other 0m 0s. SCORES Agility stayed more resilient than speed Top agility reached 61.4, while top speed peaked at 66.1. Startup quality and endurance separated more often than agility did. RAMP-UP Startup friction is the repeat problem Deep-work ramp-up ranged from 0s to 368s. That makes settling into deep work a more stable issue than any single task label.

evaluation.summary: K's report suggests a Dual-mode core, an Evening Spark time persona, and a peak flow duration around 54m 46s.

primaryPersona.name: Dual-mode
primaryPersona.summary: Shows different strengths in different contexts instead of one fixed cognitive mode. Slow-Burn Operator is the deeper performance style underneath this read.
evidence: The report keeps showing both speed-led and agility-led moments rather than one stable thinker style.
The report contains meaningful visible sessions on both sides of the speed-versus-agility split (3 speed-led, 2 agility-led).
Gets stronger after settling in and often produces the best work once the runway is long enough.
The report explicitly points to startup friction or a slower ramp-up pattern.

(Avg: 16.0-26.0) Deep Work %46.2% Proportion of session time spent in deep work bouts. (Avg: 38.0-46.8) Max Deep Work Duration316 s Longest uninterrupted deep work bout (s). (Avg: 56.9-57.5) Intensity Surges %16.7% Proportion of time with high intensity surges. (Avg: 0.0-1.0) Intrusion Rate1.0 /min Rate of entries into Fragmented state per minute. Lower is better. (Avg: 0.84-1.5) Efficiency Median59.8 Median Efficiency score. (Avg: 19.3-25.3) Efficiency Surges %60.6% Proportion of time with high efficiency surges. (Avg: 4.4-20.0) State Dynamics4.68 /min Cognitive-state switches per minute; higher means more dynamics. (Avg: 3.95-5.89) Report generated on June 22, 2026 08:46 AM2 Session Analysis Strong focus and creative immersion today. A slow ramp-up (60s) suggests it took time for your attention to 'warm up'. Top strength: Intensity Median. Area for improvement: Deep Work Ramp-Up. Quick next step: repeat what worked and aim to extend the scores by 10% in the next trial. Recommendations Insight from this session: Your balanced Cognitive Speed and Agility suggest a 'Flow' state where you are both absorbing and synthesizing effectively. Mindset to cultivate: Focus is a skill, not a switch. Consistent practice matters more than perfection.
